content.full_text:
RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION

October 23, 1995

Mr. John Slezak
1933 Smokey Ridge Avenue
Westlake Village, California 91362

Re: Your Request For Advice
Our File No. I-95-329

Dear Mr. Slezak:

We respond to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹
This advice is limited to matters within the scope of the Act; we
do not advise regarding other laws which affect the conduct of public officials. We specifically do not advise public
officials regarding incompatible activities. Inquiries as to the
application of the other laws should be addressed to your agency's
general counsel or the Office of the Attorney General.

QUESTION

Do the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Act prevent you
from serving as General Counsel of the California Department of
Transportation based on either your position as a member of the
Los Angeles Regional Water Quality Control Board or the income you
receive from the Calleguas Municipal Water District?

ANSWER

No. The Act prohibits your participation in government
decisions in which you have a financial interest. The Act does
not prevent you from holding multiple public positions, nor does
the Act prevent you from engaging in outside employment.

_____________________
1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Sections
18109-18995.

428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814 (916) 322-5660

File No. A-95-329
Page 2


FACTS

You are a member of the Los Angeles Regional Water Quality
Control Board ("LARWQCB"). You also practice law. One of your
clients is the Calleguas Municipal Water District ("CMWD"). You
are being considered for appointment as General Counsel of the
California Department of Transportation ("Cal Trans"). If
appointed to the Cal Trans position, you would like to continue to
represent CMWD in private practice. You may disassociate with
your present firm, or you may remain as "of counsel"; however,
your private practice would be limited to representing CMWD. If
you remain as "of counsel" your salary would be paid by the firm
and not by CMWD.

ANALYSIS

The Act's conflict of interest prohibition requires that a
public official disqualify himself or herself from any government
decision in which that official has a financial interest.
(Section 87100.) An official has a financial interest in a
decision within the meaning of the Act, when it is reasonably
foreseeable that the decision will materially affect: (1) the
official or any member of his immediate family; (2) any
source of income of $250 or more within the twelve months
preceding the decision; (3) any source of gifts of $280 or more
within the twelve months preceding the decision; (4) any real
property in which the official has a direct or indirect ownership
interest of $1000 or more; (4) any business entity in which the
official has an investment interest of $1000 or more; or (5) any
business entity in which the official serves as a director,
manager or employee. (Section 87103.)

Private Entity Sources of Income:

When an official has a conflict of interest in a decision,
within the above definition, he or she must disqualify himself or
herself from making, participating in making, or otherwise
attempting to influence the decision. Therefore, if you were
appointed as General Counsel to Cal Trans, and you severed your
ties to your law firm but continued to represent third parties in
private legal practice, you would be disqualified from decisions
affecting your private practice clients where required by Section
87103.

Similarly, if you retained your "of counsel" status but
limited yourself to representing the CMWD, you would be required
to disqualify yourself from decisions affecting the CMWD. This
result is compelled by the fact that your work is limited to
servicing one client. That fact indicates that although you may
not receive income directly from CMWD, your income is directly
tied to your performance for that client. It is appropriate,
therefore, that your income, though it is actually paid by the law

File No. A-95-329
Page 3


firm through which you do business, be deemed to come from that
single client which you serve. (See Mitchell Advice Letter, A-95-
308 ; Anafarian Advice Letter, No. I-90-312; Larsen Advice Letter,
No. I-89-555.)

Public Entity Sources of Income:

     Salary and reimbursement for expenses received from a
government agency are exempt from the definition of income.
(Section 82030(b).2  Accordingly, the salary you recieve from Cal
Trans as general counsel would not be disqualifying in your
position as a member of the Los Angeles Regional Water Quality
Control Board.  Similarly, any salary or reimbursement for expenses
you receive as a water board member would not be cause for
disqualification in your capacity as Cal Trans General Counsel.

Incompatible Activities, Incompatible Offices and Other Laws:

     Please note that a conflict of interest under the Act arises
only with respect to a specific decision.  There is no general
conflict of interest under the Act arising out of holding two
public agency positions.  The doctrine of "incompatible offices"
may be applicable to your facts; however, this agency does not
provide advice regarding that aspect of public agency law.

     Additionally, the Act contains no general prohibition on
outside employment for public officials.  Prohibitions on outside
employment and other incompatible activities are governed by
Government Code Sections 1125 et seq. for local agency officials
and by Government Code Section 19990 for state agency officials.
The Commission does not advise public officials regarding these
laws.

     We trust that you find this advice helpful.  If you have any
further questions regarding your responsibilities under the Act,
please direct them to this office.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Steven G. Churchwell
                                        General Counsel

                                        [illegible]

                                        By:  Daniel E. Muallem
                                             Staff Counsel, Legal Division


_______________________________

2      As you are not a salaried employee of the CMWD, the exception
for salary and reimbursement for expenses does not apply to the
income you receive from CMWD.
